title: Author + self-test advanced item types (matching/ordering/cloze/multi-select)
context: The M0 pilot bank may focus on single-select MCQ and true/false; advanced types (matching, ordering, cloze/fill-in-the-blank, multiple-select with partial credit) are specified in the grader but not yet authored in real bank items with confirmed self-test passage. This task authors example items of each advanced type in the canonical model, confirms they pass the grader self-test (including partial credit vectors), and ensures exporters correctly handle or flag them. These items also serve as golden fixtures for the validator.
objective: Author at least one complete, schema-valid, self-test-passing example item for each advanced type (matching, ordering, cloze, multiple-select with partial credit) and commit them as validated fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one complete, schema-valid item authored per advanced type: matching (pair-based), ordering (sequence), fill-in-the-blank/cloze (per-blank scoring), multiple-select MCQ (partial-credit scoring).
Each item passes the grader self-test: key→100%, all distractors/wrong orderings→0, partial-credit items→defined partial value.
Each item passes accessibility lint (alt text if assets, no color-only cues, MathML for math).
Exporters (qti-010, moodle-011) tested against these items; any unsupported type × format cells documented in the support matrix.
Items committed as golden fixtures in the validator test suite.
Human SME sign-off recorded for each item's key and rationale.
resources: quiz-bank-open-grader-002 (scoring spec — partial-credit formula for each type)
quiz-bank-open-validator-006 (CI harness — these items become golden fixtures)
output: Committed canonical-model items for each advanced type, self-test-passing, accessibility-clean, used as golden fixtures in CI and as examples for bank authors.
